description: Entries the agent found but could not fully verify due to 403 errors or missing PDF links. The user reviews this file and provides corrections, then the agent applies them.
workflow: 1. Agent finds a candidate entry but gets 403 or can't find the PDF. 2. Agent adds it here instead of rules.json. 3. User reviews and provides the correct PDF URL (or says 'remove'). 4. Next agent run reads user corrections and applies them to rules.json.

pending.blocked_reason: Found references to standing order on generative AI with disclosure requirement but could not access azd.uscourts.gov PDFs; aggregator (losey.law) confirms order exists requiring separate Notice of Use of Artificial Intelligence
pending.candidate_summary: Requires filing a separate Notice of Use of Artificial Intelligence, which discloses all language originally produced by generative AI. If any portion of a pleading has been drafted using generative AI (including ChatGPT, Harvey.AI, or Google Bard), attorneys and pro se litigants must verify that any AI-generated language was checked for accuracy using print reporters, traditional legal databases, or other reliable means by a human being.
